Control activity.evidence: A Trade Finance Manager reviews the assembled package and approves issuance. Guarantees above EUR 5 million additionally require sign-off by the Head of Trade Finance.

What it checks.evidence: No guarantee is issued without a Trade Finance Manager's approval, recorded in the Trade Finance System.

Control activity.evidence: The Compliance Analyst screens the beneficiary and the beneficiary's country against the sanctions list. A screening hit suspends the application pending Compliance investigation.

Timing.evidence: Every beneficiary is screened before issuance

What it checks.evidence: Every beneficiary is screened before issuance; the screening result is attached to the application.

Control activity.evidence: Issuance is blocked in the Trade Finance System unless available facility limit covers the guarantee amount.

What it checks.evidence: Issuance is blocked in the Trade Finance System unless available facility limit covers the guarantee amount.

Description.evidence: Applications frequently stall at the credit and facility check when the client has not pre-arranged enough limit. this is the most common reason for delay.

Root cause.evidence: Applications frequently stall at the credit and facility check when the client has not pre-arranged enough limit; M. Berger: no formal SLA on Credit team response (informally 1–2 business days) compounds the stall duration — documented as PG-BGIT-001

Description.evidence: Bespoke wording adds unpredictable delay because Legal review has no committed turnaround.

Impact.evidence: Bespoke wording adds unpredictable delay because Legal review has no committed turnaround.

What happens.evidence: M. Berger: 'bespoke wording handoff is by email today (no system handoff); for standard wording, Step 3 is effectively a no-op for the TFO'

Outputs.evidence: the screening result is attached to the application

What happens.evidence: The Compliance Analyst screens the beneficiary and the beneficiary's country against the sanctions list. A screening hit suspends the application pending Compliance investigation.

Outputs.evidence: the guarantee is transmitted to the beneficiary's bank via SWIFT. The client's facility utilisation is updated.

What happens.evidence: The Trade Finance Officer generates the guarantee instrument in the Trade Finance System and the guarantee is transmitted to the beneficiary's bank via SWIFT. The client's facility utilisation is updated.

For guarantees not fully covered by an approved facility, the Trade Finance Officer confirms that cash collateral has been received and blocked before issuance can proceed. This step is now mandatory for every partially-secured guarantee.

How it is met.evidence: The Compliance Analyst screens the beneficiary and the beneficiary's country against the sanctions list. A screening hit suspends the application pending Compliance investigation.

What it requires.evidence: The process is subject to anti-money-laundering and sanctions obligations (EU sanctions regulations, AML directives).

How it is met.evidence: M. Berger: 'Standard guarantee templates incorporate URDG 758 by reference (template clauses 1, 4, 12 specifically) and the Trade Finance System enforces template selection for standard wording; for bespoke wording, Legal confirms URDG 758 conformity as part of its sign-off at PS-BGIT-003 (now control CP-BGIT-004)'

What it requires.evidence: Bank guarantees follow the ICC Uniform Rules for Demand Guarantees (URDG 758) where the guarantee is issued on demand terms.

Why it applies.evidence: Bank guarantees follow the ICC Uniform Rules for Demand Guarantees (URDG 758) where the guarantee is issued on demand terms.

How it is met.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2013/575/oj/eng — Annex I framework requires pre-approved credit facility covering the guarantee amount — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2013/575/oj/eng — "Annex I: Classification of off-balance-sheet items ... Full-risk items: ... Guarantees having the character of credit substitutes" (CCF 100%) — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2013/575/oj/eng — Article 111: exposure value of off-balance-sheet items calculated as CCF × nominal value — fetched 2026-05-20

How it is met.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Veroeffentlichungen/DE/Rundschreiben/2023/rs_05_2023_MaRisk_BA.html — BT 1.1 Vier-Augen-Prinzip satisfied by dual-approver structure at Step 5 — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Veroeffentlichungen/DE/Rundschreiben/2023/rs_05_2023_MaRisk_BA.html — MaRisk BT 1.1 requires Funktionstrennung (function separation) between market and risk/approval functions, and Vier-Augen-Prinzip for credit decisions — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Veroeffentlichungen/DE/Rundschreiben/2023/rs_05_2023_MaRisk_BA.html — Garantiegeschäft falls under BT 1 credit business scope — fetched 2026-05-20

How it is met.evidence: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/kredwg/__1.html — KWG licence is a precondition for operating this process; internal delegation of authority governs individual issuance decisions — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/kredwg/__1.html — §1 Abs. 1 Satz 2 Nr. 2: Kreditgeschäft includes granting of loans and Garantien; regulated as banking business requiring BaFin licence — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/kredwg/__1.html — independent bank guarantees (Garantiegeschäft) fall under §1 Nr. 2 credit business — fetched 2026-05-20

How it is met.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/credit-risk/guidelines-on-loan-origination-and-monitoring — creditworthiness assessment and approval governance at Steps 2 and 5 address core EBA/GL/2020/06 requirements — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/credit-risk/guidelines-on-loan-origination-and-monitoring — EBA/GL/2020/06 requires documented creditworthiness assessment, governance and approval policies for credit facilities; applicable to guarantees as contingent credit — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/credit-risk/guidelines-on-loan-origination-and-monitoring — guarantee facilities extended to corporate clients are credit exposures in scope — fetched 2026-05-20

How it is met.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj — DORA compliance for SYS-BGIT-002 and SYS-BGIT-004 managed at infrastructure level; process-level controls rely on system availability — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj — Article 6: ICT risk management framework; Article 28: third-party ICT provider management; SWIFT is an explicitly named critical ICT provider category — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj — Deutsche Bank as credit institution is a financial entity under DORA Article 2; TFS and SWIFT are in-scope ICT systems — fetched 2026-05-20

How it is met.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/anti-money-laundering-and-countering-financing-terrorism/guidelines-on-revised-ml-tf-risk-factors — CP-BGIT-002 (sanctions screening) addresses beneficiary-level risk; alignment with trade-based ML indicator monitoring is to be confirmed with Compliance — fetched 2026-05-20

What it requires.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/anti-money-laundering-and-countering-financing-terrorism/guidelines-on-revised-ml-tf-risk-factors — EBA/GL/2021/02 identifies trade finance as higher-risk sector; institutions must apply enhanced due diligence and monitor for trade-based money laundering indicators — fetched 2026-05-20

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/regulation-and-policy/anti-money-laundering-and-countering-financing-terrorism/guidelines-on-revised-ml-tf-risk-factors — bank guarantee issuance is a trade finance product; EBA sector-specific guidance applies alongside the general AMLD obligations covered by REG-BGIT-001 — fetched 2026-05-20

In this process.evidence: The Trade Finance Officer receives the application and checks it is complete... The Trade Finance Officer confirms the client holds an approved guarantee facility... The Trade Finance Officer generates the guarantee instrument in the Trade Finance System; M. Berger: Step 3 is a no-op for standard wording; for bespoke the TFO emails draft to Legal

Responsibility.evidence: Trade Finance Officer — intake, checks, generation and delivery.

In this process.evidence: A Trade Finance Manager reviews the assembled package and approves issuance.
The Compliance Analyst screens the beneficiary and the beneficiary's country against the sanctions list. A screening hit suspends the application pending Compliance investigation.

Responsibility.evidence: Compliance Analyst — sanctions and compliance screening.

In this process.evidence: If the limit is insufficient, the application is parked and routed to the Credit team; M. Berger: Credit team can grant a temporary one-off uplift, recommend a permanent facility increase (separate process), or decline; on decline the application is formally rejected to the client through the portal

Responsibility.evidence: Credit team — limit increases when the facility is insufficient; M. Berger: can also decline leading to formal rejection

In this process.evidence: Bespoke wording is sent to the Legal team for review and sign-off before issuance.

Role in this process.evidence: A corporate client submits a Bank Guarantee application through the Corporate Portal, or via a relationship manager who keys it into the portal on the client's behalf.

Purpose.evidence: Trade Finance System — the system of record for guarantee instruments, approvals and facility utilisation.

Role in this process.evidence: Issuance is blocked in the Trade Finance System unless available facility limit covers the guarantee amount... No guarantee is issued without a Trade Finance Manager's approval, recorded in the Trade Finance System... The Trade Finance Officer generates the guarantee instrument in the Trade Finance System

Purpose.evidence: Sanctions Screening Tool — used by Compliance for beneficiary screening.

Role in this process.evidence: The Compliance Analyst screens the beneficiary and the beneficiary's country against the sanctions list.

Purpose.evidence: SWIFT — transmission of the executed guarantee to the beneficiary's bank.

Role in this process.evidence: the guarantee is transmitted to the beneficiary's bank via SWIFT.
